Privacy Preferences of CBC.

We and select advertising partners collect some of your data in order to give you a better experience through personalized content and advertising. To limit the collection of your data, please modify your privacy preferences. You can learn more in our Privacy Notice (new window).

    Featured Content

    Students, educator, suspect's family among dead in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., mass shooting

    Students, educator, suspect's family among dead in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., mass shooting

    Hundreds of people attended a candlelight vigil Wednesday evening

    LIVE
    5 minutes ago

    What we know about the teenager behind the Tumbler Ridge, B.C., mass shooting

    December 31, 1969

    Suspect's mother identified as 1 of 8 Tumbler Ridge, B.C., mass shooting victims

    December 31, 1969

    B.C. to hold day of mourning following Tumbler Ridge tragedy

    2 hours ago|
    Duration2:24

    What we know so far about the B.C. school shooting

    December 31, 1969

    Timeline: How the first 24 hours after the Tumbler Ridge, B.C., shooting unfolded

    December 31, 1969

    'What happened has left our nation in shock,' Carney says

    December 31, 1969
    New

    As schools process Tumbler Ridge shooting, officials say safety protocols need ongoing attention

    School safety protocols, threat assessments and how schools prepare for active-shooter or similar emergency situations are under a renewed spotlight following Tuesday’s devastating mass shooting in the northeastern B.C. community of Tumbler Ridge.
    Canada|11 minutes ago

    After Tumbler Ridge shooting, false claims about trans people have flourished online

    Anger and false claims are being directed at transgender people after Tuesday's mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., that left nine people dead, including the suspected shooter, who RCMP identified as an 18-year-old transgender woman.
    Canada|4 hours ago
    New

    U.S. agriculture groups launch campaign backing CUSMA trade deal

    Coalition touts benefits of CUSMA to rural America, key voting block for Trump and Republicans
    8 minutes ago
    New

    Ottawa commits to keeping Mounties on front lines, says fixing federal policing is priority

    After months of uncertainty hanging over the RCMP, Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree says the government is committed to keeping Mounties in the business of day-to-day policing across the country — while signalling that improving the federal crimes wing is his priority.
    Politics|11 minutes ago
    New

    As tensions simmer between U.S. and Iran, Iraq is feeling the heat

    Trump criticized the reappearance of former Iraqi prime minister Nouri al-Maliki
    World|9 minutes ago
    New

    Trump is set to scrap a longstanding legal basis for U.S. climate action. Here's what that could mean

    Science|11 minutes ago

    U.S. House votes against Trump's tariffs on Canada

    World|6 hours ago

    Kraft Heinz halts plans to split company

    Business|7 hours ago
    Analysis

    Gen Z toppled Bangladesh's autocratic regime. Will it decide the next government?

    World|2 hours ago

    Entertainment|2 hours ago

    It is a priority for CBC to create products that are accessible to all in Canada including people with visual, hearing, motor and cognitive challenges.

      December 31, 1969
      New

      As schools process Tumbler Ridge shooting, officials say safety protocols need ongoing attention

      School safety protocols, threat assessments and how schools prepare for active-shooter or similar emergency situations are under a renewed spotlight following Tuesday’s devastating mass shooting in the northeastern B.C. community of Tumbler Ridge.
      Canada|11 minutes ago

      After Tumbler Ridge shooting, false claims about trans people have flourished online

      Anger and false claims are being directed at transgender people after Tuesday's mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., that left nine people dead, including the suspected shooter, who RCMP identified as an 18-year-old transgender woman.
      Canada|4 hours ago
      New

      U.S. agriculture groups launch campaign backing CUSMA trade deal

      Coalition touts benefits of CUSMA to rural America, key voting block for Trump and Republicans
      8 minutes ago
      New

      Ottawa commits to keeping Mounties on front lines, says fixing federal policing is priority

      After months of uncertainty hanging over the RCMP, Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree says the government is committed to keeping Mounties in the business of day-to-day policing across the country — while signalling that improving the federal crimes wing is his priority.
      Politics|11 minutes ago
      New
